lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Woah! What's going on here? There are so many bunnies!

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: I don't know what happened, things happened so quickly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This certainly seems like a hare-y situation, where did you get them all?

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: I didn't, they just kept jumping out of my hat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean? Are you a wizard?

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: Wizard? No, I'm just a street magician, they call me The Disappointing Gelt. I was out here practicing my tricks and things went south quickly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't tell me, you were trying to pull a rabbit out of your hat and you accidentally pulled out hundreds?

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: Well... yes, some old guy gave me these magical looking stones, so I thought I'd practice my act with them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You do know these are rune stones, they're used in actual magic.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: You're chatting rubbish... real magic doesn't exist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then how did all these rabbits appear?

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: I don't know, I put the runes in my hat, said the magic words 'Rabbaradoo' and stuck my hand in and felt something fuzzy. When I pulled my hand back, I was holding a rabbit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mind if I take a look at your hat? I think there's more to this than meets the eye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well Gelt, I don't know how you've done it, but it looks like you've opened a portal.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: Please, you must help me, I beg you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I mean, I'm pretty busy... but I suppose I can find the time to help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Gelt, I need to go back.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: Please don't dawdle, I need you to fix this before the master of my order finds out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have returned.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: Oh, please tell me you managed to do something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be honest with you Gelt, it got a bit weird.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Somehow, you opened a portal to a dimension, very similar to ours, but run by rabbits instead of humans.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: That's incredible...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not even the weird bit! They live in a cult worshipping an egg, they ask it a question and whatever it says, they do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's why they all started coming through, the Duke Rabbacio asked the egg if they should jump through and it said yes.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: What the...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a lot to take in, but I've managed to stop them jumping through for now, however I couldn't close the portal.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: ...am I a god?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: Have I managed to conjure an alternate dimension with my own hands...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello? Gelt?

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: I'm the most powerful magician that has ever lived...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, you're not Gelt, you've used magic that's existed for millennia.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: You have my thanks adventurer, when I return to my order, I'll report this to our leader, Magnus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will you do with all these rabbits?

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: Since you couldn't close the portal, I'll try usher them back in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you mind letting me return to Bunbridge? I feel I have some unfinished business.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: Not a problem if you wish to return just talk to me, I'll stay for a while longer.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: Feel free to keep anything you found there as well, as a thanks from me.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: Thank you again for your help, do you wish to return to the Rabbit Realm?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I've had enough of rabbits for now.

lines.speaker: The Disappointing Gelt
lines.text: So be it, I'll be contemplating my power for a little while if you change your mind.
